long_description: The navigation computer degrades or fails in flight, causing invalid position, steering, or timing data to the mission system.
pilot_actions: Cross-check backup navigation sources, avoid reliance on coupled steering modes, switch to alternate navigation mode if available, and divert if positional confidence is lost.

long_description: The inertial navigation solution drifts in flight, causing growing position and attitude errors that corrupt steering cues and sensor alignment.
pilot_actions: Cross-check with alternate navigation references, avoid precision weapon or intercept tasks, and recover before navigation uncertainty exceeds mission limits.

long_description: External satellite navigation input is lost in flight, reducing navigation update quality and increasing long-term inertial error growth.
pilot_actions: Continue on inertial or backup references, avoid precision-dependent tasks, and recover if mission navigation accuracy is no longer sufficient.

long_description: Navigation alignment quality degrades or corrupts in flight, causing unstable reference data to the mission and display systems.
pilot_actions: Treat navigation outputs as suspect, avoid coupled modes or precision attack tasks, and recover using verified backup references.

long_description: Navigation time-reference distribution fails in flight, degrading sensor correlation, track timing, or networked avionics consistency.
pilot_actions: Simplify sensor employment, distrust tightly coupled timing-dependent functions, and terminate mission tasks that require synchronized avionics timing.
